description:
This image displays a four-panel comparison of spatial land cover data resulting from "Test production" and "Current production" methods, applied to two distinct geographic areas. The two rows represent different areas, identified by coordinate-like labels on the left: `3957750 3114334` (top row) and `3984094 3150073` (bottom row). The two columns compare the "Test production" (left) with the "Current production" (right).

In the upper geographic area (`3957750 3114334`), the "Test production" panel shows a high density of blue patches and numerous scattered red patches, all set against a background of finely detailed green land cover features. The corresponding "Current production" panel for this area exhibits significantly fewer blue and red patches, with the green land cover features remaining prominent.

In the lower geographic area (`3984094 3150073`), the "Test production" panel displays a high density of red patches and some scattered blue patches, also against a green land cover background. The "Current production" panel for this area shows a high density of red patches, comparable to the test production, but with noticeably fewer blue patches.

Across all panels, the green colour consistently represents a widespread background land cover type. There is no legend, scale bar, compass, data source, or reference year visible in the image. The visual differences between "Test production" and "Current production" imply an evaluation or comparison of distinct feature detection or classification outcomes from the two methods.
